disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mucosa (mouth, eyes, lips) clear; no fever; no 'punched-out' eroded vesicles (against eczema herpeticum/SJS)
trigger_question_examples: Any sores/blisters in your mouth or on your lips, or red painful sticky eyes?
Do you have a fever, or clusters of small punched-out sores?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No mouth or lip sores, my eyes are fine, no fever, and no clusters of punched-out sores.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Superficial peeling/scaling crusts (not thick honey-coloured impetigo crusts, no purulence); harsh shampoos aggravating
trigger_question_examples: Are the crusts thick and honey-coloured with pus, or superficial and scaly?
Have harsh shampoos/scrubbing made it worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're superficial and scaly, yellowish but not thick honey-coloured or pus-filled, and harsh shampoos make it worse.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a flesh-eating infection spreading to my eyes?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure that a superficial, mucosa-sparing, afebrile peeling eruption is not a flesh-eating infection or SJS; explain likely a superficial autoimmune blistering skin condition (often misread as seborrhoeic dermatitis), advise stopping harsh shampoos and starting a topical steroid + emollient, arrange dermatology confirmation, and give an eczema-herpeticum/mucosal/infection safety-net.
